summary: Recent updates to the **libwebrtc integration** in Firefox have enhanced the browser's ability to handle video and audio more efficiently during real-time communications. Notably, the integration of an H.264 FMTP (Format Parameter) parser allows for improved support of this widely used video codec, which is crucial for delivering high-quality video over the internet. Additionally, improvements to the Media Capabilities framework now provide more accurate support queries based on media resolution and framerate, enabling better performance optimization. These changes are significant as they result in a smoother experience for users engaging in video calls and other media-intensive activities, leading to **better quality and reliability in WebRTC (the technology behind video calls)**.
